Giratina

#0486 Regigigas #0488 Cresselia
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Giratina is a Ghost/Dragon type Pokémon introduced in Generation 4.

Altered Forme Origin Forme

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0487
Type Ghost Dragon
Species Renegade Pokémon
Height 4.5 m (14′09″)
Weight 750.0 kg (1653.5 lbs)
Abilities 1. Pressure
Telepathy (hidden ability)
Local № 0210 (Platinum)
0237 (Legends: Arceus)

Training

EV yield 3 HP
Catch rate 3 (0.4% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 0 (lower than normal)
Base Exp. 340
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Undiscovered
Gender Genderless
Egg cycles 120 (30,584–30,840 steps)

Base stats

HP 150
410 504
Attack 100
184 328
Defense 120
220 372
Sp. Atk 100
184 328
Sp. Def 120
220 372
Speed 90
166 306
Total 680 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Evolution chart

  • Giratina does not evolve.

Giratina changes

  • In Generation 4, Giratina (Altered Forme) has a base experience yield of 220.
  • In Generation 4, Giratina (Origin Forme) has a base experience yield of 220.
  • In Generations 5-7, Giratina (Altered Forme) has a base experience yield of 306.
  • In Generations 5-7, Giratina (Origin Forme) has a base experience yield of 306.

Pokédex entries

Altered Forme

Diamond
Pearl		 A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.
Platinum It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.
HeartGold
SoulSilver		 This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.
Black
White
Black 2
White 2		 It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.
X
Omega Ruby		 This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.
Legends: Arceus There is one Hisuian verse that tells of a powerful light creating a deep shadow. I imagine that this deep shadow is Giratina.

Origin Forme

Legends: Arceus Giratina loses its legs upon changing into this form. I believe this Pokémon must hail from a world where the heavens and the earth are as one, though I have no way of proving it.

Moves learned by Giratina

  • In other generations

Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Giratina does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Giratina cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by level up

Egg moves

Giratina does not learn any moves by breeding in Pokémon Scarlet & Violet.

Answers to Giratina questions

  • What is a good moveset for Giratina?
  • Is Giratina the devil Pokemon ?
  • Why did the Aether House mention Bronzong instead of Dialga?
  • What does Giratina represent?
  • If you Knock Off/Thief a Origin Form Giratina's Griseous Orb, will it revert to Altered Form in the middle of battle?
  • Where is "the Great Hole" and how do you get to the Bottom In EOT
  • Advice for defeating Giratina in Explorers of Time?
  • How does Terrible Might affect Giratina's stats in PLA?
  • What is percentage of power dialga-palkia-gitatina-get from their gems?

Other languages

English Giratina
Japanese ギラティナ (Giratina)
German Giratina
French Giratina
Italian Giratina
Spanish Giratina
Korean 기라티나 (giratina)
Chinese (Simplified) 骑拉帝纳
Chinese (Traditional) 騎拉帝納

 

English Renegade Pokémon
Japanese はんこつポケモン
German Rebellen-Pokémon
French Pokémon Renégat
Italian Pokémon Ribelle
Spanish Pokémon Renegado
Korean 반골포켓몬
Chinese (Simplified) 反抗宝可梦
Chinese (Traditional) 反抗寶可夢

Name origin

girasol
a red opal
platina
an alloy of platinum, the game of which it is the mascot
